sourceMovie.title: Priscilla
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Priscilla (2023) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: (500) Days of Summer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Priscilla clicked for you, (500) Days of Summer (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Revolutionary Road
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Priscilla clicked for you, Revolutionary Road (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Priscilla?
faq.answer: Try (500) Days of Summer, Revolutionary Road, Cruel Intentions—each shares DNA with Priscilla while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Priscilla?
faq.answer: Priscilla (2023) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Priscilla worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
